bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just beginning (t=0.0) and I am making the first bid, I will start by proposing the outcome that maximizes my utility. My preferred value across all issues is 'valueC', so I will request this to establish a strong opening position.

The negotiation has just begun (t=0.02), and the opponent's opening offer yields the lowest possible utility for me, despite their emotional appeal. I will ignore the opponent's message as cheap talk, hold firm on the high-weight issues (issueF, issueE, issueC, issueD) which are critical for my overall utility, but offer a minor concession on issueA (from valueC to valueB) to demonstrate a willingness to negotiate.

The current time is very early in the negotiation (t=0.04). The opponent has repeatedly offered their maximum sequence resulting in a very low utility for me (0.2), while using emotional appeals as a tactic. I will continue to slowly concede on my lowest-weight issues to signal willingness to negotiate without significantly damaging my utility. Having already conceded slightly on issueA, my next step is to concede on my other lowest-weight issue, issueB, offering valueB, while holding firm on the high-weight issues C, D, E, and F.

I am targeting a utility of 0.88 by conceding fully on issueA (my lowest-weight issue) and partially on issueB, while holding firm on my higher-weight issues C, D, E, and F. The opponent has stonewalled three times with identical, maximal-demand offers accompanied by emotional appeals, making no genuine attempt to find a middle ground. By granting them their exact requested value on issueA, I am providing a clear signal to encourage them to start reciprocating and making concessions.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.08), and the opponent has not made a single concession, instead repeatedly sending the same offer combined with emotional appeals. Since I have already conceded on issueA and issueB, I will fully concede my lowest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB) to their preferred value to demonstrate a willingness to cooperate. However, I will strictly hold firm on my heavily weighted issues (issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF).

The opponent has repeatedly sent out emotional appeals, which I treat as cheap talk, yet they finally made a small concession on issueD in their latest bid. To show cooperation and encourage further movement safely while maintaining a very high utility for myself, I will match their concession on issueD (offering valueB). I will hold firm on issueC, issueE, and my highest-weight issueF (valueC), as we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.1).

We are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.12). The opponent makes persistent emotional appeals to mask their aggressive holding pattern, having conceded only issueD to valueB so far. To maintain momentum and gently provoke further concessions, I am willing to concede issueC down to valueB while firmly protecting my highest-weighted issues (issueF and issueE).

The time fraction is still only 0.14, so it is way too early to make massive concessions, particularly when the opponent is holding almost completely firm on their initial values and relying heavily on emotional appeals instead of matching my compromises. To show flexibility without sacrificing further utility, I will offer a lateral tradeoff by reducing my expectation on issueE (from valueC to valueB) while slightly demanding more on issueC (from valueB back to valueC). This maintains my overall utility right around 0.78 while testing which issues the opponent might value more.

The opponent is using emotional appeals and asserting severe constraints to push for `valueA` across all issues, but they recently conceded first `issueD` to `valueB` and now `issueF` to `valueB`. Since it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I have ample time before needing to make drastic concessions. However, to foster progress and show flexibility, I will match their latest concession on `issueF` while revisiting their previous openness to `valueB` on `issueD`, keeping my highest priority values intact on `issueC` and `issueE`.

The opponent continues to rely entirely on emotional appeals while holding most issues at their preferred positions, having only swapped their concession from issueD to issueF in recent turns. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), there is no need to make sweeping concessions. I will accept their latest concession on issueF ('valueB') while proposing a new combination of concessions on issueD and issueE, maintaining a strong overall utility for myself (~0.74) while testing their flexibility.

I treat the opponent's repeated appeals to "desperation" and "livelihood" as cheap talk and negotiation tactics. Looking at the objective bidding history, they have shown flexibility by offering `valueB` on `issueC`, `issueD`, and `issueF` at different turns. Since `issueF` and `issueE` are my highest priority issues, I will hold firm on `valueC` for them at this early stage (t=0.2). To show reciprocity and inch closer to an agreement, I will adopt their recently offered `valueB` for `issueC` and their previous `valueB` for `issueD`, while keeping `issueA` and `issueB` (my lowest priorities) at their preferred `valueA`.

The opponent is aggressively using emotional appeals and claiming desperation, but I will treat these as non-binding cheap talk. Their bidding behavior shows they are willing to make minor concessions scattered across issueC, issueD, and issueF at different times. At t=0.22, it is still early enough to keep my utility around 0.78. Since I previously held firm on issueE, I will introduce a concession on issueE (to valueB) to test if this aligns better with the opponent's true hidden preferences, while maintaining my highest-weighted issue (issueF) at valueC.

The opponent's relentless emotional appeals are accompanied by scattered concessions in their offers, recently giving up `valueC` on `issueD` and previously yielding ground on `issueC` and `issueF`. Given it is still early (t=0.24), I can afford to target a fairly high utility while locking in their concessions. I will accept their generous offer of `valueC` on `issueD`, incorporate their past concession of `valueB` on `issueC`, concede slightly on `issueE` to `valueB`, and maintain `valueC` on my highest-priority `issueF` to secure a utility around 0.78.

The current time fraction is relatively early (t=0.26), so maintaining a high utility around 0.85 is ideal. The opponent's messages rely heavily on emotional appeals of desperation, which I will treat as cheap talk while focusing on their actual bids. They have previously shown flexibility on issues C, D, E, and F in different rounds (e.g., issueD at valueC in t=0.24, issueE at valueB this round), so I will propose an offer capturing valueC on issues C, D, and my most important issue F, while conceding on issueE to their recent valueB, and keeping issues A and B at their preferred valueA.

The opponent has just conceded on issueF (my highest weight issue), offering valueC, and previously showed flexibility on issueD (offering valueC). At `t=0.28`, I will craft an offer that locks in valueC on issueF and issueD, maintaining high overall utility for myself (~0.86). To show cooperation, I will retain valueA on issues A and B where my utility weights are low, and also meet them on issueC with valueB which they've previously been willing to offer.

The opponent is consistently using emotional appeals claiming financial distress, which I will treat as cheap talk. By observing their previous offers, they have occasionally conceded on issueF, issueE, and issueD, indicating they might be more flexible on these issues. Since the time fraction is only 0.3, I will maintain a high target utility (around 0.90) by securing `valueC` on my most important issues (issueD, issueE, issueF) while signaling mild flexibility on my lower-weight issues like issueA and issueC to see if we can approach a mutually acceptable trade-off.

I am targeting a utility around 0.86, accommodating your strong preference for valueA on my least important issues (A and B). While your messages rely heavily on emotional appeals and fabricated desperation, your bidding history clearly shows flexibility, having previously offered valueC on issues D and F. I am willing to completely yield on issues A and B while slightly compromising on C, but I will maintain my positions on my higher-weighted issues where you've already shown you can meet me.

I observe that the opponent has consistently held firm on `issueB` while using emotional appeals, so I will concede `valueA` on that issue since it holds the lowest weight for me. They have, however, signaled willingness to accept `valueB` on `issueA`, `issueC`, and `issueE`, as well as `valueC` on `issueD` in previous rounds. Ignoring their dramatic messages as cheap talk, I am proposing a combination that integrates these past concessions from them while securing my top priorities (`issueD` and `issueF`), maintaining a strong utility for myself at this mid-early stage.

At t=0.36, I am in the early-to-mid stage of the negotiation, so keeping my target utility relatively high (~0.85) is optimal. The opponent uses persistent emotional appeals which I disregard as cheap talk, analyzing strictly their bidding behavior. They have shown clear flexibility by offering 'valueC' individually on issues C, D, and F in separate past offers, so I will combine these valueC positions with concessions on my lowest-weight issues (A and B) at their preferred 'valueA' to test for a mutually viable tradeoff.

The opponent is continuously using emotional appeals in their messages, accompanied by slow concessions. In their latest turn, they proposed `valueB` for `issueC` and `valueC` for `issueD`, signaling flexibility there. I will incorporate their latest concessions on `issueA`, `issueB`, `issueC`, and `issueD` entirely to show cooperation, while holding firm on `issueE` and `issueF` which hold the highest utility for me, in an attempt to reach a compromise since the deadline is still far away.

The opponent is consistently making emotional appeals to hide the fact that their offers yield very low utility for me, barely hovering above 0.5 total utility. At t=0.40, they conceded to 'valueB' on issues C, E, and F, indicating some flexibility on those issues. I will accept their concession on issue E to 'valueB', but since it's still relatively early I will stand firm on getting 'valueC' for my highest-weight issues (F, D, C) while maintaining my concession to 'valueA' on my lowest-priority issues (A, B) to test their responsiveness.

The opponent continues to rely on strong emotional appeals, but their numerical bids show a significant shift, offering me `valueC` on issues D and E in their latest bid. Recognizing this, I will accept their exact offer on issues A, B, C, D, and E, but I will hold firm on my highest-priority issue (issueF), requesting `valueC`. This secures a high utility of 0.78 for me while conceding heavily to the opponent's stated needs to push us closer to an agreement before the mid-game gives way to the pressure of the deadline.

The opponent consistently bids `valueA` for issues A and B while using emotional appeals about their difficult situation. I have conceded on A and B since they are my lowest-weight preferences (0.1 weight each). The opponent recently showed a willingness to accept `C: valueC` and `E: valueB` (at t=0.44), and previously `D: valueC` and `F: valueC`. I will offer a compromise that meets their demands on A, B, and E, while securing my top-utility values for C, D, and my most important issue, F (0.3 weight). This targets a strong utility of 0.862 while demonstrating willingness to find common ground.

The opponent's strategy primarily leverages emotional appeals while making sporadic concessions across different issues, particularly recently offering valueC on `issueD` and valueB on `issueC` and `issueE`. Being close to the midway point (`t=0.46`), I will accommodate their latest concessions on `issueC`, `issueD`, and `issueE`, as well as their persistent requests on my low-weight issues `issueA` and `issueB`. I will continue to hold firm on my most important issue, `issueF`, as well as `issueD` which they have already proven willing to concede, keeping my utility around 0.78.

The opponent's messages consist of emotional appeals and claims of desperation ("painful sacrifices," "desperate"), but their actual bidding history reveals an active exploration of trade-offs, particularly conceding on my highest-priority `issueF` to `valueC` in their latest offer. To capitalize on this, I will accept their latest values for `issueC` and `issueD` (`valueB`), maintain my concession on my low-weight `issueA` and `issueB` (`valueA`), and insist on `valueC` for `issueE` and `issueF` (where they have shown prior flexibility). This keeps my utility at a robust 0.784 while closely matching the shape of their recent proposals.

I am positioned at the midpoint of the negotiation time. While the opponent consistently uses emotional appeals in their messages, I treat these as cheap talk and focus exclusively on their bidding patterns. They have started offering substantial concessions, specifically 'valueC' for my second-most important issueE and 'valueB' for issueB in their latest bid, while having offered 'valueC' for my top priority (issueF) just before that. I will construct an offer that captures these known accepted values, keeping issueA at valueA while securing 'valueC' on my highest-priority issues (issueE and issueF) and maintaining solid mid-tier utilities on the remaining issues.

The opponent is consistently using emotional and desperate messages, which I am disregarding as cheap talk. Focusing on the numbers, the opponent recently conceded `issueF` to `valueC` and previously offered `issueE` to `valueC` and `issueB` to `valueB`. I will construct a bid targeting ~0.74 utility by locking in these highest-utility values for myself on `issueE` and `issueF` while conceding slightly on `issueC` and `issueD` to find a workable combination.

The opponent's consistent emotional appeals are classic cheap talk, so I will focus entirely on their bid history, which shows they are willing to yield on my highest priority issues at different times (e.g., issueF at t=0.52 and issueE at t=0.50). Given we are halfway through the negotiation (t=0.54), I will propose a tradeoff that secures my top utilities on issues E and F, and grants the opponent their preferred or recently offered values on my lower-priority issues (A, B, and C). I will offer valueB for issueD and issueA to bring my total utility to a solid mid-game level (~0.82) while demonstrating flexibility.

The opponent's messages are composed of consistent, non-binding emotional appeals, which I will treat as cheap talk. They have, however, finally conceded significantly on my highest-weight issues (issueE and issueF) in their latest bid. I will reciprocate this by fully conceding on my least important issues (issueA and issueB to their preferred 'valueA'), while asserting strong values on the medium-weight issues (issueC and issueD) to maintain an appropriate utility of 0.862 for this mid-game phase.

I am currently at t=0.58. The opponent's natural-language messages have consistently been emotional appeals indicating "desperation," which I treat as cheap talk while relying on their actual bidding behavior. In their latest bids, they demonstrated a willingness to offer `issueE: valueC` and `issueF: valueC` (at t=0.56) and most recently `issueB: valueC` and `issueD: valueB` (at t=0.58). To bridge the gap, I will assemble these latest concessions into a combination that retains my most critical utility values on issueE and issueF while dropping on my lower-priority issueA.

The opponent's text messages consist of persistent, increasingly desperate emotional appeals which are non-binding tactics; I will rely strictly on their offer history. They have shown willingness to concede on my most important issues, previously offering `issueE: valueC` (at t=0.56), `issueC: valueC` (at t=0.60), and `issueF: valueC` repeatedly. I am now offering a combination of their own concessions on C, E, and F while yielding on issueA, issueB, and issueD to reach a mutually acceptable agreement while still securing a high utility (0.782) for myself.

The opponent's messages rely heavily on emotional appeals and feigned desperation, which I treat as cheap talk. However, their physical bids show genuine flexibility, particularly by recently offering valueC on issueC and valueB on issueA. Since I previously conceded on issues B and D (my lower-weight issues), I will accept their latest movement on issueA and issueC while holding firm on my most critical issues (E and F) for which they have also historically shown some flexibility.

The opponent's messages consist largely of emotional appeals, which I will ignore as non-binding cheap talk. Instead, I observe their bidding behavior, where they have recently signaled an acceptance for 'valueC' on issueC, issueD, and previously issueF—my highest assigned priorities. To advance toward a mutually agreeable deal before the deadline, I will concede on my lower-weighted issues (A, B, and E) by adopting values they have actively requested in recent rounds, while firmly locking in my top utility on the most profitable issues.

The opponent's continuous emotional appeals contrast with their ongoing concessions, which now include offering 'valueC' on issueF and issueB in their latest bid. I will capitalize on these values and assemble a combination of recent opponent concessions (such as issueD at valueC and issueE at valueB) that still protects my overall utility on my heavily weighted issues. At this time fraction, a targeted utility just above 0.85 offers a realistic path toward agreement without yielding prematurely.

The opponent's messages rely heavily on emotional appeals and claims of desperation which I treat as non-binding cheap talk. I will focus strictly on their bidding patterns. Recent offers show the opponent is willing to accept valueC for issues C, D, and F, valueB for issue E, and valueB for issue B. I am proposing a combination of these previously offered values to maintain a high utility for myself (~0.90) while offering terms they have explicitly put forward in the past few rounds.

The current time fraction is 0.70, so we are entering the late stage of the negotiation where finding an overlapping agreement is critical. The opponent's message relies on an emotional appeal, but their bidding behavior shows willingness to concede to `valueB` on several issues (A, B, C, E), while preserving their top preferences elsewhere. I will match their latest offer on issues A, B, C, and E, substituting `valueC` on my highest-weight issues (D and F), which they have conceded to in the past (e.g., at t=0.68).

The latest offer from the opponent matches my preferences on issue C and F, which are highly critical. Their messages consistently employ emotional appeals such as "practically begging" and "back against the wall," which I read as cheap-talk attempts to extract concessions under fake pressure. To maintain momentum and secure an agreement as we enter the later stages of the negotiation, I will accept their proposal on issues A, B, C, D, and F but ask for a slight compromise on issue E to find a middle ground yielding an acceptable utility above 0.8.

The time fraction is 0.74, and the deadline is steadily approaching. The opponent's messages rely almost entirely on emotional distress and have repeatedly included the exact same narrative, which I treat as cheap talk. In their latest offer, the opponent conceded heavily on issues C and D while holding on E and F. Since my preference heavily weights issue F, I will retain F at my top value (valueC) and E at valueB, while fully matching the opponent's most recent offer on issues A, B, C, and D. This allows me to secure a high utility (0.903) while demonstrating clear cooperation that directly builds off their concessions.

The opponent's relentless emotional appeals are pure negotiation tactics, but their actual offers show a willingness to concede on my most important issue (issueF) and recently on issueA. Since we are in the late game (t=0.76), I will lock in their concessions on issueF and issueA, and accommodate their strong preference for issueB=valueA. I am offering a balanced compromise on the mid-weight issues (issueD and issueE) to target a strong mutual agreement with a utility of roughly 0.86 for myself.

The opponent's recent bid introduced new shifts: offering valueC on issueB and valueB on issueE while consistently trying to claim issues they likely value highly (A, E, or F). Their messages consist heavily of emotional and desperation tactics which I am treating as cheap talk. Seeing that the time is at 0.78, I will propose an offer that keeps my most valuable issues (issueF and issueC at valueC) while matching their recent proposal on issueA, issueB, and issueE to yield an acceptable mutual tradeoff around ~0.86 utility for myself.

The opponent's messages rely heavily on emotional appeals and feigned desperation, but their recent bidding history shows they are willing to accept my preferred values for several issues (they offered valueC for issueC and F recently, and valueB for E). At t=0.80, I need to bring us closer to an agreement. I will match their exact t=0.80 offer on issues A, B, C, and D, while holding firm on valueB for issueE and valueC for issueF, both of which they have signaled they can accept in recent turns.

The opponent's messages consist of a repeated emotional appeal and false urgency, which I will treat as cheap talk. However, their actual bids have shown a willingness to concede on issueB (valueC) and issueD (valueC), which they included in their most recent offer. I will incorporate those concessions into a new package, while holding firm on values that give me strong utility (issueC at valueC) and offering a middle-ground (valueB) on issueE and issueF to maintain my utility around 0.86 as we approach the final stages of the negotiation.

The opponent's emotional appeals remain intense, portraying extreme desperation, but their bidding pattern shows concrete movement. In the last two offers, they have interchangeably conceded on issueD (`valueC` at t=0.82) and issueF (`valueC` at t=0.84). To maximize my utility while moving towards an agreement near the deadline, I will combine their recent concessions, locking in `valueC` for issueF (my highest weight) and issueB, while matching their preference for issueE (`valueA`) and issueC (`valueB`). This offer maintains a high utility for me (~0.82) while incorporating terms they have already proven willing to accept.

The deadline is approaching (t=0.86) and the opponent's messages are heavily reliant on emotional appeals, suggesting they may be reaching their reservation limit on some issues. I will accept their latest position on issues A, B, C, D, and E (valueB, valueC, valueC, valueA, valueB), which they just proposed at t=0.86, while holding firm on my most important issue, F (valueC), which they recently conceded to at t=0.84. This provides me a solid utility of 0.823 while extending a highly favorable compromise on 5 out of 6 issues.

The time fraction is 0.88 and the opponent relies heavily on emotional appeals in every message, which I treat as non-binding cheap talk. Their latest offer introduces valueC for issues A, B, and D, suggesting these are possible compromises, but asks for valueA on the remaining issues. I will attempt a compromise by accepting valueA for issueE (where they have shown strong resistance) and offering valueC for my most important issue (issueF), while balancing the remaining issues to target an acceptable utility above 0.8.

Given that we are entering the final stage of negotiation at t=0.90, securing an agreement to avoid the reservation value of 0 is crucial. The opponent's latest offer provides me with an exceptionally high utility of 0.86 by granting my preferred 'valueC' on my most heavily weighted issues (F, E, and C). To formally agree and solidify this highly favorable outcome, I am mirroring their last offer exactly.
